title: Operations Associate
requirements_met: Track record of building things/side projects: built multiple AI agents (competitor intel, growth experiments, career matching) + active GitHub
Fast execution and self-direction: first employee at Minima, ran multiple workstreams in early-stage chaos
Comfort with unglamorous operational work: Founder's Associate role brought structure to early-stage chaos
Structural thinking and problem-solving: built metric dashboards, ran failure analysis at Amazon Alexa
Clear written and verbal communication: PR, content, investor updates
Low ego, high agency mindset: took ownership of whatever was necessary across functions
gaps: Role notes 6-7 day in-office cadence in London - intense commitment from Cambridge
preferred_met: Early-stage startup experience: 6 years scaling Minima seed to Series A
Background in high-intensity environments: startup scaling + Amazon
Proficiency using AI to increase output: builds AI agents with Claude Code

experience_fit.calculation: Role open to any level incl new grads; user mid-level early-stage operator is an exact fit for the operator profile = 15

industry_match.calculation: Corgi is an AI financial infrastructure company - genuine AI company = 15

location_match.calculation: London in-person; user open to in-person London = 10

salary_penalty.reason: GBP 50-70K range overlaps user GBP 50K minimum

notes: Corgi Insurance (YC S24): AI financial infrastructure company vertically integrating insurance - designs, underwrites, prices, issues insurance directly using AI. AI-native operator.

must_haves.req: Track record of building things / side projects
must_haves.note: Builds AI agents with Claude Code (competitor-intel, growth-experiments); $4.5m community presale built from scratch

must_haves.req: Move fast and execute without instructions
must_haves.note: First employee at Minima; turned ambiguous founder priorities into workstreams

must_haves.req: Comfort with unglamorous ops work (chasing vendors, logistics, HMRC)
must_haves.note: Managed PR agencies, internal updates, cross-functional execution; FA role was exactly this

must_haves.req: Structural thinking — messy problems to actionable plans
must_haves.note: Built metric dashboards, segmented 50k email list, Alexa failure analysis
Content engine, PR (2m views), investor updates
First employee took ownership of whatever was needed across functions

met_strengths: Role explicitly open to ANY experience level incl new grads — no years gate
No insurance/regulatory domain requirement for candidate (insurance is the company, not a skill gate)
Bonus points: early-stage startup + high-intensity background + AI-to-enhance-output — Ryan hits all three
interview_match.one_line_verdict: Best fit of the cluster: every must-have met, no domain/years gate, three bonus-points hit; only friction is the 6-7 day in-office cadence (lifestyle, not a screening blocker).
